tldr: Lenny Martinez (Bahrain Victorious) took the final stage on the Côte du Linguador in a two-up sprint with Jonas Vingegaard, who finished second same time and sealed the overall. Tejada third at +7s; the GC closed exactly where Stage 5 had set it.

narrative.headline: Lenny Martinez wins stage, Vingegaard wins GC
narrative.body: 143 km, hilly Nice circuit, the Côte du Linguador as the day's decisive climb. Vingegaard and Lenny Martinez gapped the chasers on the climb and rode the final kilometres two-up; Martinez took the stage, Vingegaard the overall by more than four minutes.

gcImpact.headline: Vingegaard wins Paris-Nice 2026
gcImpact.body: Vingegaard the overall winner — career-rebooting GC win, his first major after the 2024 Itzulia crash. Daniel Martínez 2nd at +4:23, Steinhauser third.

sources.title: Paris-Nice 2026 Stage 8 — Martinez wins, Vingegaard GC
